req.title: Fraud alert response
req.statement: A customer shall confirm or deny a suspected-fraud alert within 24 hours; unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-013 defines three distinct behavioral paths: (1) a customer confirms fraud within 24h → alert closes and card is reissued; (2) a customer denies fraud within 24h → alert closes, no reissue; (3) the alert expires (24h elapsed, unacknowledged) → account auto-freezes and any subsequent PATCH returns 410. Test design covers: EP for valid confirm/deny responses, STC for the active→closed state transition on confirm, DT for the confirm-vs-deny decision branch, BVA for the 24h expiry boundary, and NEG for the 410 expired-alert error path. Each test uses a fresh alert fixture to guarantee idempotency.

tests.name: test_confirm_fraud_alert_closes_alert_and_reissues_card
preconditions: A fraud alert exists in ACTIVE state for the test account
The alert was created less than 24 hours ago
expected: Response body contains status=closed and response=confirm
A new card record exists with status=pending_reissue or issued
trace: R-FIN-013: customer confirms suspected-fraud → alert closed; card reissued if fraud
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_confirm_fraud_alert_closes_alert_and_reissues_card(
    active_alert_fixture,   # <fixture>: yields {"alert_id": str, "account_id": str}
    auth_headers,           # <fixture>: yields {"Authorization": "Bearer <token>"}
    api_base_url,           # <fixture>: yields base URL string
):
    alert_id = active_alert_fixture["alert_id"]
    account_id = active_alert_fixture["account_id"]

    patch_resp = requests.patch(
        f"{api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json={"response": "confirm"},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert patch_resp.status_code == 200
    body = patch_resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "closed"
    assert body["response"] == "confirm"

    get_alert = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}", headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert get_alert.json()["status"] == "closed"

    cards_resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/cards",
        params={"account_id": account_id},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert cards_resp.status_code == 200
    cards = cards_resp.json()["cards"]
    reissued = [c for c in cards if c["status"] in ("pending_reissue", "issued")]
    assert len(reissued) >= 1, "Expected at least one reissued card"

tests.name: test_deny_fraud_alert_closes_alert_without_card_reissue
preconditions: A fraud alert exists in ACTIVE state for the test account
The alert was created less than 24 hours ago
actions: GET /cards?account_id={account_id} to verify no new reissue
expected: Response body contains status=closed and response=deny
No card record with status=pending_reissue created after denial
trace: R-FIN-013: alert closed on any acknowledgement; card reissued ONLY if fraud confirmed
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_deny_fraud_alert_closes_alert_without_card_reissue(
    active_alert_fixture,
    auth_headers,
    api_base_url,
):
    alert_id = active_alert_fixture["alert_id"]
    account_id = active_alert_fixture["account_id"]

    cards_before = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/cards",
        params={"account_id": account_id},
        headers=auth_headers,
    ).json()["cards"]
    reissue_ids_before = {c["id"] for c in cards_before if c["status"] == "pending_reissue"}

    patch_resp = requests.patch(
        f"{api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json={"response": "deny"},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert patch_resp.status_code == 200
    body = patch_resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "closed"
    assert body["response"] == "deny"

    cards_after = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/cards",
        params={"account_id": account_id},
        headers=auth_headers,
    ).json()["cards"]
    reissue_ids_after = {c["id"] for c in cards_after if c["status"] == "pending_reissue"}
    new_reissues = reissue_ids_after - reissue_ids_before
    assert len(new_reissues) == 0, f"Unexpected card reissue on deny: {new_reissues}"

tests.name: test_patch_expired_alert_returns_410
preconditions: A fraud alert exists that has already expired (created >24h ago, unacknowledged)
Account is auto-frozen as per R-FIN-013
expected: Response body contains an error code indicating alert expiry
trace: R-FIN-013 error pathway: 410 when alert expired
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_patch_expired_alert_returns_410(
    expired_alert_fixture,  # <fixture>: yields {"alert_id": str} for a >24h-old alert
    auth_headers,
    api_base_url,
):
    alert_id = expired_alert_fixture["alert_id"]

    resp = requests.patch(
        f"{api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json={"response": "confirm"},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 410, (
        f"Expected 410 for expired alert, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    error_body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in error_body or "code" in error_body, (
        "Response body must contain an error descriptor"
    )
    error_text = str(error_body).lower()
    assert any(kw in error_text for kw in ("expired", "gone", "410")), (
        f"Error body does not reference expiry: {error_body}"
    )

tests.name: test_alert_auto_freezes_account_at_24h_boundary
preconditions: A fraud alert was created exactly 24 hours ago (boundary instant)
The alert has NOT been acknowledged by the customer
Account freeze job has run (simulated or real)
actions: GET /accounts/{account_id} to check freeze status after 24h boundary
Attempt PATCH /alerts/{id} with {"response": "deny"} to confirm expiry
expected: PATCH returns 410, confirming the alert is expired at the boundary
trace: R-FIN-013: unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account (24h boundary)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_alert_auto_freezes_account_at_24h_boundary(
    boundary_alert_fixture,  # <fixture>: yields {"alert_id": str, "account_id": str}
                             #   alert age == 24h, freeze job already executed
    auth_headers,
    api_base_url,
    trigger_freeze_job,      # <helper_fn>: forces the auto-freeze scheduler to run
):
    alert_id = boundary_alert_fixture["alert_id"]
    account_id = boundary_alert_fixture["account_id"]

    trigger_freeze_job(account_id)  # ensure scheduler ran at/after 24h mark

    account_resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/accounts/{account_id}",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert account_resp.status_code == 200
    assert account_resp.json()["status"] == "frozen", (
        "Account must be frozen when alert unacknowledged at 24h boundary"
    )

    patch_resp = requests.patch(
        f"{api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json={"response": "deny"},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert patch_resp.status_code == 410, (
        f"Expected 410 at 24h boundary, got {patch_resp.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_invalid_response_value_returns_422
preconditions: A fraud alert exists in ACTIVE state
expected: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity (or 400 Bad Request) is returned
Response body describes the validation error
trace: R-FIN-013: only confirm or deny are valid responses; invalid values must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_invalid_response_value_returns_422(
    active_alert_fixture,
    auth_headers,
    api_base_url,
):
    alert_id = active_alert_fixture["alert_id"]

    patch_resp = requests.patch(
        f"{api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json={"response": "maybe"},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert patch_resp.status_code in (400, 422), (
        f"Expected 400/422 for invalid response value, got {patch_resp.status_code}"
    )

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "active", (
        "Alert must remain active after a rejected invalid response"
    )
